verses.text: Israel is called to sing aloud with trumpet and timbrel at the appointed feasts, a statute God ordained. Joyful worship is commanded. God Himself appoints set times for His people to praise Him with gladness.

Israel is called to sing aloud with trumpet and timbrel at the appointed feasts, a statute God ordained. Joyful worship is commanded. God Himself appoints set times for His people to praise Him with gladness.

God recalls easing the burden from their shoulders and answering when they cried in trouble. He rehearses past rescue. Remembering how God delivered before stirs fresh trust and thanks.

God recalls easing the burden from their shoulders and answering when they cried in trouble. He rehearses past rescue. Remembering how God delivered before stirs fresh trust and thanks.

'Hear, O my people,' He pleads; let no strange god be among them, for He is the LORD who brought them up: 'open thy mouth wide, and I will fill it.' He invites large expectation. God delights to satisfy the soul that comes to Him with bold faith.

'Hear, O my people,' He pleads; let no strange god be among them, for He is the LORD who brought them up: 'open thy mouth wide, and I will fill it.' He invites large expectation. God delights to satisfy the soul that comes to Him with bold faith.
But His people would not listen, so He 'gave them up unto their own hearts' lust.' Refused mercy ends in abandonment. The saddest judgment is to be left to the desires we would not surrender.
But His people would not listen, so He 'gave them up unto their own hearts' lust.' Refused mercy ends in abandonment. The saddest judgment is to be left to the desires we would not surrender.

'Oh that my people had hearkened!' God laments; He would have subdued their foes and fed them with 'the finest of the wheat.' Disobedience forfeits blessing. God's grief over our refusals shows how much good our sin makes us miss.
